titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brian Kiss photographs of stained glass in Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University
Kiss (Brian) Photographs of stained glass in Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 30, 2024 at 04:03

did.unittitle: Brian Kiss photographs of stained glass in Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University
did.abstract: The collection consists of digital photographs (eighty .tiff files) of close-up images of the stained glass medallion windows located in the Sterling Memorial Library Nave.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 46-B (YRG 46-B), Records and materials documenting individual Yale buildings, facilities, and grounds.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of the creator(s), copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Brian Kiss Photographs of Stained Glass in Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University (RU 1136). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The photographs for this collection were donated by Brian Kiss, 2014.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of digital photographs (eighty .tiff files) of close-up images of the stained glass medallion windows located in the Sterling Memorial Library Nave.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Originally from Shelton, Connecticut, Brian Kiss has been an employee of Sterling Memorial Library since 2006. His interest in photography began in high school and has focused on music and fashion photography. Following the opening of the Front Line Services Desk in the Nave in 2014, Brian grew enamored of the stained glass created by G. Owen Bonawit in the 1930s and lent his photographic skills to capturing the beauty of these newly restored pieces of art.
The eighty donated images are a collection of his favorite works, shot close up to capture the detail of each piece. His favorite, "Monumental Gratitude" depicts a boat being tossed on stormy waters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Digital photographs are arranged as a single format series, and original order has been maintained. No further arrangement work has been done.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was captured by Manuscripts and Archives staff in 2014 from a flash drive for preservation and access. The flash drive was not retained. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Brian Kiss photographs of stained glass in Sterling Memorial Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research.

did.unittitle: Poorvu Center for Teaching and Learning, Room 117, staff offices
Poorvu Center for Teaching and Learning, Room 118B (formerly workspace for the Cataloging Department)
Poorvu Center for Teaching and Learning, Rooms 120 & 121 (formerly 117B, Acquisitions and Shipping Room)
Poorvu Center for Teaching and Learning staircase near York St. Entrance, Room 116A (formerly Catalogue Department Office)
Room 107M, staff lunchroom (known as The Spoon Room)
Room 116A, Bibliographical Press (now office of the Director of Communications for the Yale University Library)
Room 116C, Office of Director of Finance & Administration
Room 116D, Office of the Yale University Library Copyright Librarian and Contracting Specialist
Room 117A, Office of the Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
Room 119M, Library Communications and Development Office (all images from Amman, Stande und Handwerker)
Room 120, entrance to Music Library and Circulation
Room 135, Office of the Building Maintenance Supervisor
Room 136, Lecture Hall
Room 138, Memorabilia Room
Room 139, Periodical Reading Room (formerly the Newspaper Reading Room)
Room 144, Exhibition Corridor
Rooms 147 and 150, Manuscripts and Archives anteroom and exhibition room
Room 152, Reception room for Administrative Offices
Room 155, Associate University Librarian's Office
Room 157, University Librarian support staff office
Room 160, Office of the University Librarian
Room 165, Linonia and Brothers Room
Room 175, Digital Humanities Lab (formerly the Franke Periodical Reading Room)
Room 180, Vestibule to the International Room
Rooms 182A and 184A, Exhibition Corridor including area for Privileges and Front Line Services staff and glass facing Selin Courtyard
Room 207, East Asia Library Group Study Room, Romance Languages Study
Room 207A Romance Language Study (now librarian's office)
Room 210 Romance Language Seminar (now librarian's office)
Room 211, DSHRS Special Collection
Room 212, Special Collection (now office of the Librarian for Chinese Studies)
Rooms 213A, 213B, 213C, East Asian Librarian offices
Room 214, Southeast Asia Reading Room, and 214A
Room 215, Andrews Study, and 215A
Room 218, Cooper Collection (now a meeting room)
Room 219, East Asia Reading Room, Yale Collection of American Literature
Room 221, East Asia Reading Room, Fielding Collection
Room 222, East Asia Reading Room, English Seminar
Room 223, Germanic Seminar (now the office of the Associate University Librarian for Scholarly Resources)
Room 224, Speck Collection (now the office of the Yale Library Director of Development)
Room 228, The Franklin Papers Drama Collection
Rooms 229, 230, 231, and 232, The Franklin Papers
Room 310, History Seminar (now an office)
Room 311, History Study (now the African & Latin American Collections)
Room 313, Orientalia Seminar (now Librarian for Latin American Studies)
Room 314, Orientalia Study (now part of the Babylonian Collection)
Room 315, History Study (now Southeast Asia Reading Room)
Room 316, Political and Social Science Study (now an office)
Room 317, Political and Social Science Study (office of the Director of Library Security)
Room 318, Political and Social Science Seminar (now Babylonian Collection)
Room 319, Law Seminar (now Babylonian Collection)
Room 322, History Seminar (now the Babylonian Collection)
Room 323, Babylonian Collection (now a Babylonian Collection classroom)
Rooms 324, 325, 326, and 327, Babylonian Collection
Room 329, American Oriental Society (formerly Egyptology Reading Room)
Room 331A, Special Collection (formerly the Boswell Papers)
Room 331B, Special Collection (now the Fortunoff Archive)
Room 331C, Special Collection (now the Fortunoff Archive)
Room 331D, Special Collection (now unoccupied office)
Room 333, Far Eastern Collection
